title: Align bundle shape with schema
lines: Ensure top-level keys are exactly: schema_version, conversation, context, tools, runtime, raw.
Move framework-specific payload into raw instead of adding extra root fields.
Verify conversation.system and conversation.user are non-empty strings.
rationale: Most bundle validation failures come from missing required fields or extra root keys.

title: Fix JSON before finalize
lines: Use the tool args JSON editor and ensure it parses to an object.
Return only valid JSON for tool arguments.
Include only required fields expected by the tool schema.
rationale: Schema parse and argument-shape errors are deterministic and resolved by valid JSON.

title: Reduce output size
lines: Keep responses brief.
If calling a tool, return only JSON.
Do not include explanatory text with JSON.
Include only required arguments.
rationale: Shorter structured output lowers truncation risk under token limits.

title: Separate prose from tool JSON
lines: Use TOOL_CALL mode with JSON args only.
Move explanations into notes instead of draft output.
Keep canonical tool envelope free of prose.
rationale: Mixing prose with structured envelopes commonly breaks downstream parsers.

title: Clarify tool schema
lines: Provide a non-empty JSON schema object for each tool.
Declare required fields explicitly in the schema.
Avoid placeholder schemas like {} for production gating.
rationale: Ambiguous or empty schemas prevent reliable argument validation.

title: Improve context coverage
lines: Add the missing facts as retrieved_snippets before execution.
Include source identifiers to improve traceability.
Limit assumptions and record them explicitly in notes.assumptions.
rationale: Insufficient context frequently causes unstable tool choices and weak outputs.

title: Resolve instruction conflicts
lines: Prioritize a single source of truth for response rules.
Remove contradictory constraints between system and developer prompts.
Document tie-breaker logic in the developer prompt.
rationale: Conflicting instructions cause non-deterministic behavior in agent outputs.
